steps.title: Use a common denominator
steps.content:
The equation is a sum of reciprocals, so use the common denominator $17pqs$ on the right side before combining the terms.
$$
\frac{1}{p}=\frac{17qs}{17pqs},\quad \frac{1}{q}=\frac{17ps}{17pqs},\quad \frac{1}{s}=\frac{17pq}{17pqs},\quad \frac{1}{17}=\frac{pqs}{17pqs}
$$

steps.title: Combine the reciprocals
steps.content:
Add the fractions on the right side.
$$
\frac{1}{R}=\frac{17qs+17ps+17pq+pqs}{17pqs}
$$

steps.title: Take the reciprocal
steps.content: Solving for $R$ gives $$ R=\frac{17pqs}{17qs+17ps+17pq+pqs} $$ The answer is B.
